body:
Title: CRA Form TL2 2025 Claim for Meals and Lodging Expenses.

Sources:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/tl2/tl2-25e.pdf
- https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/publications/t4044/employment-expenses.html

CRA Form TL2 2025 calculates meals and lodging expenses for transport employees and long-haul truck drivers. Line 3 subtracts non-taxable meal amounts received or receivable from Part 2A meals, floors the result at zero, and multiplies by 50%. Line 6 subtracts non-taxable meal amounts received or receivable from Part 2B eligible long-haul truck driver meals, floors the result at zero, and multiplies by 80%. Line 9 adds lodging and shower expenses from Part 2A line 7 and Part 2B line 8. Line 11 subtracts non-taxable lodging amounts from line 9 and floors the result at zero. Line 12 adds lines 3, 6, and 11 and is entered on T1 line 22900.

The TL2 employment conditions state that meal and beverage expenses for long-haul truck drivers during eligible travel periods are deductible at 80%, while other transportation employee meal claims use the 50% rate. TL2 defines a long-haul truck as a truck or tractor designed for hauling freight with gross vehicle weight rating of more than 11,788 kilograms. TL2 defines an eligible travel period as at least 24 hours away from the municipality or metropolitan area for driving a long-haul truck transporting goods at least 160 kilometres from the employer's establishment. Guide T4044 describes the simplified meal method rate as 23 dollars per meal.

Numeric grounding: TL2 line 3 rate is 0.50. TL2 line 6 rate is 0.80. T4044 simplified meal rate is 23 dollars per meal. TL2 long-haul minimum is 24 hours. TL2 long-haul distance is 160 kilometres. TL2 long-haul truck threshold is more than 11788 kilograms gross vehicle weight rating.
